title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Graph Lens: Gita Fetch Negotiation Prune Tags
summary: A mirror, fork, upstream, deployment remote, and namespace-specific integration ref disagree after a rewrite; one stale client may overwrite reviewed work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita fetch negotiation prune tags increment in distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook

steps.title: Predict Gita Fetch Negotiation Prune Tags
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G33 is a theory in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

# Worked focus: gita-fetch-negotiation-prune-tags — Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
